channel.title: Posts on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: 🚀 How to Retrieve Stale Branches Across All Repositories in a GitHub Organization
item.description:
Keeping your GitHub repositories clean is crucial for maintainability. Over time, branches pile up, making it difficult to track what’s relevant. If you’re managing multiple repositories in an organization, manually identifying stale branches can be a hassle.

Luckily, with the GitHub CLI (gh), you can automate this process and generate a report of all non-main branches along with their last authors.

This guide walks you through a Bash script that:
✅ Retrieves all repositories in an organization
✅ Lists branches, excluding main, master, and azure-master
✅ Identifies the last commit author for each branch
✅ Groups the results by author for better visibility

item.title: 🔹 Azure Storage Redundancy: Ensuring Data Availability and Durability
item.description:
Azure Storage automatically stores multiple copies of your data to protect against failures, power outages, and even massive natural disasters. Redundancy ensures that your data remains available and durable even when failures occur.

This guide covers all redundancy options available in Azure Storage and how they impact data durability, availability, and failover scenarios.

🌍 Primary Region Redundancy

Azure Storage always maintains three copies of your data in the primary region. There are two replication options:
